**Wojciech Trocki \- Employee Development Plan \- From Sept 2025**

* See [guide](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1R-uhAX_vpAQ3xn2p0p22wb7pF3pArsrGfK8fcGoug-k/edit) for detailed guidance on how to complete this and a sample plan.   
* See [L\&D Course Catalog](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1sPAZlQIKrOLoW4NfuDNbNiILQxRSJ0APgY2NSWHKYME/edit) for learning opportunities to support your development goals. More info: [L\&D Wiki](https://wiki.corp.mongodb.com/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=100269300)   
* [P\&G FY25 Year End - Wojciech Trocki](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1BrC-sfUkLy7XBZILtOzuyKXvCJ8Cbi8ZLBU09qXeYh8/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.404t7tmuuzt1)  
* [P\&G FY26 Year-End - Wojciech Trocki](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Y6_V6uUv8Y_Tgz1t-UXSz1PaPSqfJtx79tfthsW7zAo/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.2bluhyunz4vh)  
* [P\&G FY26 Mid-Year - Wojciech Trocki](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1jxAjAKZVzQDecq9lrOSnupBtwaeM2iDo4P1-blrbz7g/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.1fe87qbo7408)  
* [Self Reflection: P\&G Mid 25- Wojciech Trocki](https://docs.google.com/document/d/12ro6kAdaoKMlC-4FZ7yUCndR5gofUAhAKuBWNySerSo/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.404t7tmuuzt1)

Updated: Feb 12, 2025

| Long-Term Aspiration *Identify your career aspirations. It does not have to be a specific role.* |
| ----- |
| **I want to operate as a Staff-level technical leader who: Owns major, reliability-sensitive platform initiatives end-to-end (e.g., Inbound SSO, Remote MCP), from ambiguous problem space to safe rollout and long-term operations. Is known for clear, evidence-backed communication and fast, high-quality decisions, ensuring stakeholders can rely on my recommendations without needing to re-verify assumptions. Scales impact through public artifacts and durable documentation, defaulting to shared spaces over direct messages (DMs) so cross-team partners (IAM, DevTools) have clear context. Drives tools to full adoption, ensuring pragmatic AI and platform patterns (like Evergreen MCP) are deeply embedded into developer workflows, not just shipped as standalone resources.**  |

| Northen Stars for this Period  |  |
| ----- | :---- |
| **Skills, Behaviors & Knowledge** | **Communicate with clarity backed by evidence** Default to **public docs/threads** for decisions, risks, and recommendations; avoid DM-only context. Treat any statement others might act on as a **claim that needs citable evidence** (logs, dashboards, tickets, design docs) or explicitly label it as a **hypothesis** with a validation plan. Use structured formats (TL;DR, Why/What/How, 1‑pagers, decision memos) so stakeholders can restate my recommendation in one sentence. Slow down initial replies in high‑stakes threads to reduce self‑corrections and fragmented updates. **Make good decisions quickly in ambiguous, cross‑team work** Apply **one‑way/two‑way decision thinking** and push decisions to the right level (close to data / DRIs) while keeping accountability. Close discussions with a **clear recommendation, decision, and next step**, even when there is disagreement. Be explicit about whether I’m sharing **ideas vs. recommendations vs. decisions** in reviews and design discussions, to reduce decision latency. **Focus and prioritisation (Major / Minor / Elective discipline)** Limit active scope so my **Major** gets ≥50% of my time, my **Minor** \~25%, and **Electives** stay within \~15–20% self‑learning bandwidth. Make trade‑offs visible to managers and partners, instead of silently over‑committing. Say “no” or “not now” more often to protect focus on agreed priorities. **Turn exploration into durable outcomes** For AI/experimentation, ensure each exploration has a **clear hypothesis, success criteria, and adoption path** (e.g., Evergreen MCP pattern, IPA bot work) rather than remaining as isolated demos. Measure and **evidence adoption / impact** (where possible) or explicitly frame outcomes as mixed and capture what I learned. **Customer‑obsessed, product‑driven engineering** Stay close to internal and external customers (Support, SRE, IAM, Growth, Reddit/StackOverflow) and bring those insights into designs and rollouts. Keep asking **“what problem are we solving for whom?”** before pushing a technical solution, especially in reliability‑sensitive work (rate limiting, Remote MCP, V1 EOL, IAM auth gaps).  |
| **Opportunities** | **Communication clarity & assumption validation** Reduce instances where unvalidated assumptions are presented with confidence (e.g., Bucket4j, OAuth/Postman‑like situations). Build a habit of attaching **source links or evidence** to any adoption/impact claim, and reconcile those with 360/stakeholder feedback when results are mixed. **Visible, shared decision‑making** Move more decision‑making into **public artifacts**: memos, RFCs, P\&G‑style docs, and Confluence pages, instead of 1:1s and DMs. Use clearer **status / intent labels** in docs and threads (e.g., DRAFT / SEEKING INPUT / DECISION PROPOSED / DECISION MADE). **Right‑sizing rigor and scope** Apply full rigor (deep docs, extensive experiments) only where risk demands it; use lighter‑weight approaches for lower‑risk, reversible work to maintain momentum. Be more intentional about **not fully packing weeks**; leave capacity for unplanned but high‑leverage Staff‑level work. **Adoption and integration follow‑through** For things like pocket guides, Evergreen MCP, CNCP docs, or AI guidelines, ensure there is a **plan to embed them into team workflows** (e.g., runbooks, onboarding, team rituals) and inspect usage with partners (Support, DevTools, CNCP). |
| **Relationships** | **Staff:[Sai Sundar Raghavan](mailto:sai.raghavan@mongodb.com) Help with onboarding Collect testimonials ![][image1] IAM** [Jeremy Bensing](mailto:jeremy.bensing@mongodb.com)Estabilish growth/collaobration What areas of IAM we can improve and influence. ![][image2]  [Jamel Charouel](mailto:jamel.charouel@mongodb.com)Estabilish growth/collaobration Growth team culture **Remote MCP** [Bianca Lisle](mailto:bianca.vianadeaguiar@mongodb.com)Estabilish growth/collaobration Mentorship![][image3] [Ciprian Tibulca](mailto:ciprian.tibulca@mongodb.com)Technical Evaluation for Auth/ Devtools[Gagik Amaryan](mailto:gagik.amaryan@mongodb.com)/[Himanshu Singh](mailto:himanshu.singh@mongodb.com)/[Melanija Cvetic](mailto:melanija.cvetic@mongodb.com) Helped and onobarded them to the REMOTE MCP SERVICE. CNCP team ([Ev Agner](mailto:everton.agner@mongodb.com), [Bruce Coddington](mailto:bruce.coddington@mongodb.com)) |

| Concrete Action Plan  |  |
| ----- | :---- |
| **Actions** | Build habit of growth not induced by delivery but by extra activities like continuous reading. Include time each week covering book reading. Seek general knowledge and share insights on the channels Create dedicated focus time to work on gaols Build habit on blog posting covering things I learned  I want to build public presence to build my own brand outside mongodb. MongoDB period created hole in my career without any public visibility: [https://pl.linkedin.com/in/wojciech-trocki](https://pl.linkedin.com/in/wojciech-trocki) how we can fit that hole by public presence.  Build habbit of focused work avoiding multiple threads and confusion Reduce frequent slack browsing. Reduce non intentional engagements during engineering syncs  Build practical Operational experience Across Atlas. Work thru Oportunities to provide clear and targeted impact on operational excellence and rollout strategies |
| **Opportunities** | **IAM**: Building structured plans and working on rollout of the entire Authentication Logic for multi month engagements. **Mentoring:** [Sai Sundar Raghavan](mailto:sai.raghavan@mongodb.com)with his future gateway work. **Working** with [Agustin Bettati](mailto:agustin.bettati@mongodb.com)on AI champions program 20% focus on Remote MCP operational experience and growth. Assisting [Ciprian Tibulca](mailto:ciprian.tibulca@mongodb.com)with Operational aspects of MCP. Technical documents reviews |
| **Relationships** | **Keep 1:1s with[Sai Sundar Raghavan](mailto:sai.raghavan@mongodb.com)[Justin Richer](mailto:justin.richer@mongodb.com)[Ciprian Tibulca](mailto:ciprian.tibulca@mongodb.com)[Bianca Lisle](mailto:bianca.vianadeaguiar@mongodb.com)[Agustin Bettati](mailto:agustin.bettati@mongodb.com)** |

| Values / Leadership Principles Pick 1-3 values (MongoDB’s or your own) or leadership principles that will be most important for you to embody as you work toward your goals. These values and/or principles will be your north star over the next 6 months as you seek to develop against the plan you laid out above.   |  |  |
| ----- | :---- | :---- |
| **Leaders communicate with clarity** – Directly targets my top growth area around assumption validation, decision closure, and keeping collaboration transparent and easy to follow. Leaders make good decisions quickly – Ensures my technical strengths scale in cross‑team work by combining speed with better evidence and explicit decisions. Leaders focus on outcomes – Helps me avoid over‑committing and reinforces the Major/Minor/Elective discipline and SMART goals you’re already trying to use.  | **Leaders communicate with clarity –** Directly targets my top growth area around assumption validation, decision closure, and keeping collaboration transparent and easy to follow. Leaders make good decisions quickly – Ensures my technical strengths scale in cross‑team work by combining speed with better evidence and explicit decisions. Leaders focus on outcomes – Helps me avoid over‑committing and reinforces the Major/Minor/Elective discipline and SMART goals you’re already trying to use.  | **Leaders communicate with clarity –** Directly targets my top growth area around assumption validation, decision closure, and keeping collaboration transparent and easy to follow. Leaders make good decisions quickly – Ensures my technical strengths scale in cross‑team work by combining speed with better evidence and explicit decisions. Leaders focus on outcomes – Helps me avoid over‑committing and reinforces the Major/Minor/Elective discipline and SMART goals you’re already trying to use.  |

| Obstacles List any barriers that could prevent you from achieving your goals and your plan to overcome them. Obstacles can be either internal or external. Tip: Think about what feels challenging, or what puts you outside your comfort zone.  |  |
| ----- | :---- |
| **API champions/Slack notifications heavy workloads** | **Adjustment to teams in 2 timezones creates continuous engagement and always online**  |
| **Mitigation**: Work with team to validate expectations and level of engagements. Seek to close out engagements that taking more than 1 month timeframe. | **Mitigation**: More focus times where I close everything including slack/emai.  |

| Current Goals Make a commitment about when you’ll revisit this plan, and pick 2-4 goals you will have completed at that time.  The goals are like the tickets for the action plan items \- what goals can I complete to get closer to completing the overarching action plan? Tip: When you pick your goals, aim to select goals covering 3 points of impact. [For example](https://www.mongodb.com/company/leadership-principles): self-learning, gaining credibility, helping others become more productive, how can I have a cross-cutting impact. ## **Private Growth Tracker (SMART Goals Execution)**

### **✅ Done (Recently Completed)**

* **\[Operational Readiness\] Remote MCP Launch Readiness:**  
  * **Specific:** Lead the operational readiness for Remote MCP by creating a runbook, Launch Readiness Assessment (LRA) guidelines, and influencing the IAM team's rollout strategy.  
  * **Measurable:** Delivered 1 recorded team demo, finalized SSO Project Notes, and structured AI-assisted test cases for authenticator discovery APIs.  
  * **Achievable:** Leveraged my system knowledge to bridge gaps between Auth and Remote MCP.  
  * **Relevant:** Directly supports my Major focus (Inbound SSO/Remote MCP) and demonstrates Staff-level end-to-end ownership.  
  * **Time-bound:** Completed prior to Remote MCP launch.  
* **\[External Storytelling\] Lead Dev & Internal Brand Building:**  
  * **Specific:** Share insights from the Lead Dev Conference and establish my internal brand regarding AI, career growth, and community engagement.  
  * **Measurable:** Published 1 Lead Dev Report and hosted 1 Friday demo covering AI achievements, my personal growth path, and MongoDB Reddit engagement.  
  * **Achievable:** Utilized existing team syncs (Friday demos) to maximize reach.  
  * **Relevant:** Addresses the "Elective" focus of external storytelling and scaling impact.  
  * **Time-bound:** Completed in February 2026\.  
* **\[Architecture\] Infrastructure Threat Modeling & Fern Research:**  
  * **Specific:** Deepen understanding of existing infrastructure by conducting a Threat Model review, and research/implement sticky sessions with Fern.  
  * **Measurable:** Provided documented feedback in Slack on the Threat Model and documented the sticky sessions approach.  
  * **Achievable:** Scope was contained to specific review cycles and targeted Fern POCs.  
  * **Relevant:** Builds the technical authority required for Staff-level architecture decisions.  
  * **Time-bound:** Completed Q1 FY26.

### **🔄 Current (In-Flight)**

* **\[Customer Empathy\] API Champions "Customer Zero" Testing:**  
  * **Specific:** Use self-learning time to test recent API Champions features (3rd party integrations, backup flows) on my personal Atlas cluster.  
  * **Measurable:** Identify and document specific friction points, upgrade paths, and feedback to improve the end-to-end API experience.  
  * **Achievable:** Uses existing Atlas test clusters and allocated elective time.  
  * **Relevant:** Directly supports the Minor focus (API Champions) by providing evidence-backed user feedback.  
  * **Time-bound:** Ongoing throughout Q1, delivering feedback before follow-up engagements.  
* **\[Platform Expansion\] CNCP & Node.js Documentation:**  
  * **Specific:** Contribute to the Cloud Native Control Plane (CNCP) documentation to enable future Node.js CNCP development.  
  * **Measurable:** Publish Node.js CNCP docs and provide a documented list of issues to the CNCP team to improve their process.  
  * **Achievable:** Aligns with my current platform knowledge and technical writing skills.  
  * **Relevant:** Scales impact through durable documentation (addressing P\&G feedback).  
  * **Time-bound:** Expected completion by end of current sprint cycle.

### **📋 Backlog (Future Goals)**

* **\[Technical Authority\] Establish Sentry/Alerting Expertise:**  
  * **Specific:** Establish myself as the domain expert for Alerting and Sentry within the platform team to drive operational excellence.  
  * **Measurable:** Audit existing alerts, duplicate/align existing initiatives to Jira for better tracking, and present a unified alerting strategy.  
  * **Achievable:** Will build upon the foundation set by the Remote MCP runbooks.  
  * **Relevant:** Aligns with my long-term aspiration to own reliability-sensitive platform initiatives.  
  * **Time-bound:** Target initiation in Q2 FY26.  
* **\[Workflow Optimization\] Best-in-Class AI Practices:**  
  * **Specific:** Champion a project to formalize AI-assisted workflows (standardized PRs, structured Jira tickets) to reduce manual sludge between tools.  
  * **Measurable:** Create a demonstrably successful workflow integration that tracks active usage across 1+ partner teams.  
  * **Achievable:** Expands upon my success with Evergreen MCP.  
  * **Relevant:** Directly addresses P\&G feedback regarding "Adoption & Integration."  
  * **Time-bound:** Target proposal draft by Q3 FY26.
